entities.name: Badminton
entities.description: A racquet sport played using rackets to hit a shuttlecock across a net.

entities.name: Racket
entities.description: The implement used to hit the shuttlecock in badminton.

entities.name: Shuttlecock
entities.description: A feathered projectile used in badminton, known for its high drag and top speed.

entities.name: Net
entities.description: The horizontal bar that separates the two halves of the court in badminton.

entities.name: Singles
entities.description: A form of badminton where one player competes against another on each side.

entities.name: Doubles
entities.description: A form of badminton where two players compete against two other players on each side.

entities.name: Court
entities.description: The rectangular area on which badminton is played, divided by a net.

entities.name: Aerobic stamina
entities.description: Physical endurance required for sustained aerobic exercise in badminton.

entities.name: Agility
entities.description: The ability to move quickly and change direction, essential in badminton.

entities.name: Strength
entities.description: Muscular power required for hitting the shuttlecock with force in badminton.

entities.name: Speed
entities.description: The ability to move and react quickly, crucial in badminton.

entities.name: Precision
entities.description: Accuracy in hitting the shuttlecock with control in badminton.

entities.name: Motor coordination
entities.description: The ability to coordinate movements of the body, essential for playing badminton.

entities.name: Sophisticated racquet movements
entities.description: Advanced techniques involving wrist flexibility in badminton.

entities.name: Battledore and shuttlecock
entities.description: An earlier game from which badminton developed, played with a racquet and a shuttlecock.

entities.name: Duke of Beaufort's Badminton House
entities.description: The origin of the name 'badminton', located in Gloucestershire.

entities.name: Poona or Poonah
entities.description: The garrison town where the game was particularly popular and rules were first drawn up.
